name: Canvas Overlay
description: Full-bleed spatial shell with a primary canvas, floating toolbar overlays, and presence/status surfaces that sit above the canvas without consuming layout width.

header.right_content: Secondary actions, view toggle, or collaborator summary. Keep controls compact so they do not dominate the canvas.
header.button_sizing: Use compact controls in the overlay header: py-1.5 px-3 text-sm (~32px tall). Avoid oversized default button chrome.

body.note: Primary full-bleed canvas lives here. This is not a card surface and should not inherit page-local shell padding.

overlay.pointer_events: none by default; interactive panels opt back in
overlay.note: Toolbar, presence indicators, and floating inspectors live above the canvas. These overlays must not consume permanent layout width.

guidance.shell_spacing: Canvas-overlay owns the viewport edge insets for floating controls. Page components should place the canvas content and overlay surfaces only, not wrap them in extra shell-width containers or centered cards.
guidance.overlay_surfaces: Floating inspectors and toolbars may use d-surface or theme decorators, but the base canvas should remain borderless and full-bleed.
guidance.mobile_behavior: On narrow widths, collapse secondary overlay panels into bottom sheets or compact floating pills rather than shrinking the canvas itself.

code.imports: Target-specific imports vary. Translate the internal_layout contract into your framework's router outlet, canvas component, and overlay primitives.
code.example: CanvasOverlayShell(root) -> Canvas(body as full-bleed viewport) + OverlayHeader(compact actions) + FloatingToolbar + PresenceIndicators + OptionalInspectorSheet
